title: 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple - Critic and Audience Scores
text: 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple received strong critical reception with a Tomatometer score of 92% based on 344 reviews. The critics' consensus describes it as a direct continuation of 28 Years Later that ups the gore while deepening the dread, finelyadored by Nia DaCosta's unnerving direction as well as Ralph Fiennes and Jack O'Connell's inspired performances. The Popcornmeter audience score reached 88% based on over 2,500 verified ratings. The audience summary notes the film is as hard to look at as it's hard to look away from, a mad, visceral mood piece. Several reviewers gave the film an A rating, calling it an absolute blast despite its disturbing content. Another noted that the movie's willingness to embrace absurdity makes it the best iteration of the rage virus series. However, not all critics were positive, with one reviewer giving it a C rating, suggesting the screenplay reeks of desperation.
